hero.headlineLineOne: You can sketch a building in five minutes.
hero.headlineLineTwo: Getting it approved still takes five months.
hero.subheadline: Planning Matrix maps your full permit path in two questions — authorities, documents, specialist reports. Recommended by the system. Released by your certified architect.
hero.trustline: In pilot with architecture firms and developers in Thuringia and Saxony.

problem.heading: You can design a building in five minutes. Everything around it, you can't.
problem.block1: Volume, area, cost — automatic. AI renders — trivial. Creativity is no longer the bottleneck.
problem.block2: Today, you commission a full planning process just to find out which permits you actually need, which documents go to which authority, which specialist reports you must obtain.
problem.block3: Three questions. Six months. Before a single stone is laid.

product.heading: Three steps. From plot to permit path.
product.step1Body: You answer two questions: where do you want to build, what do you want to build? Nothing more.
product.step2Body: The system maps your project against German planning and building law — and produces a complete roadmap of permits, documents, and specialist reports.
product.step3Body: Your certified architect reviews the recommendation. Only their release turns the suggestion into a plan.

mockups.capturePrompt: What do you want to build?

domains.heading: How the system thinks.
domains.subheading: Every German building permit lives across three legal domains. Planning Matrix maps all three.
domains.tabAquestion: What may be built at this site?
domains.tabAbody: Bebauungsplan, Flächennutzungsplan, § 34 BauGB. Which use, which scale, which height — and who decides at the municipal level.
domains.tabBbody: Landesbauordnung, setbacks, fire safety, parking. Which construction requirements apply — and which evidence must be filed with the application.
domains.tabCbody: Heritage protection, environment, water, emissions, energy. Which specialist authorities are involved in parallel — and at which point in the process.

demo.heading: From intention to roadmap. In two answers.
demo.q1: Where will the project be built?
demo.q2: What do you want to build?

audience.heading: Three perspectives. One system.
audience.card1Body: You understand early what your project actually costs — not in euros, but in permits, reports, and time. Before you engage your first architect.
audience.card2Body: The system does the research. You do the judgment. Every recommendation is your release — fully traceable, always reviewable.
audience.card3Body: You plan portfolios, not single projects. One permit roadmap per plot, one click to compare locations side by side.

trust.heading: Recommendation is not decision.
trust.body1: Planning Matrix is not legal advice. The system recommends — your certified architect releases. Every recommendation is provenance-tracked: which statute, which source, which version. Every transition is auditable.
trust.body2: What is ultimately submitted to the Bauamt carries a human seal. Not "AI-powered approval." AI-assisted preparation — confirmed by a person.
trust.pillarOneBody: Every recommendation tagged with statute, source, version, and reasoning.
trust.pillarTwoBody: Every transition from suggestion to release fully logged.
trust.pillarThreeBody: Submission authority stays with the person — not the tool.

pricing.heading: Pricing model in development.
pricing.body: Planning Matrix is preparing for public launch. We are working with pilot partners on a pricing model that fits the reality of construction projects — not standard SaaS tiers. To shape it with us, talk to us.

faq.heading: Short, honest answers.
faq.a1: No. Planning Matrix is a recommendation system. Every recommendation is reviewed and released by a certified architect — only then does it become the basis of your application. Only the authority itself can give legally binding information.
faq.a2: We are starting in Thuringia and expanding state by state. Which state comes next depends on the availability of the respective state building codes and on local pilot partnerships.
faq.q3: Who is liable for the recommendations?
faq.a3: Responsibility for the submitted application sits — as it does today — with the submitting architect. Planning Matrix documents the statutes and sources behind every recommendation. That provenance is part of the audit trail.
faq.q4: Do I still need my own architect?
faq.a4: Yes. Submission authority in Germany is bound to the person, not the tool. If you don't yet have an architect, we can introduce certified partners from our network on request.
faq.q5: How does Planning Matrix handle my data?
faq.a5: All data is processed and stored in the EU, GDPR-compliant. Project data is used solely to generate your roadmap — never for model training or third parties.
faq.a6: The pricing model is being shaped with pilot partners. We will publish it once it holds up. Early users get preferred terms — talk to us.

finalCta.heading: Build with foresight.
finalCta.body: Early access is limited. We are working directly with pilot users in Thuringia and Saxony first.

notFound.heading: This page does not exist.
notFound.body: Maybe it was moved — or just a typo. No problem.

wizard.title: New project · Planning Matrix

cancelDialog.title: Discard your answers?
cancelDialog.body: Your answers so far will be discarded and you'll return to the dashboard.

q1.headline: What would you like to build?
q1.sub: Pick the option closest to your project.

unsureExplanation.neubau_einfamilienhaus: A fully new residential building with one or two units — detached or part of a row-house cluster.
unsureExplanation.neubau_mehrfamilienhaus: A new residential building with three or more units — usually a higher building class with stricter fire-protection requirements.
unsureExplanation.sanierung: Modifying an existing building: energy upgrades, floor-plan changes, adding a storey. Often a notification rather than a full permit.
unsureExplanation.umnutzung: Repurposing an existing property — for example commercial to residential or vice versa. Typically triggers a fresh building-law assessment.
unsureExplanation.abbruch: Planned demolition of an existing building. Notification under Art. 57 (5) BayBO; additional approvals if heritage-protected.
unsureExplanation.sonstige: A different project. We'll start with the standard single-family-home template and adapt it in conversation.

q2.headline: Do you already have a plot?
q2.sub: If yes, enter the address or parcel reference. If no, we'll proceed with assumptions.
q2.addressHelper: We use the address to research the development plan and local statutes.
q2.addressInvalid: Please enter a full address with house number and postal code.
q2.noPlotNotice: Note: without a plot we can't yet assess zoning (Area A) or other provisions (Area C). We start with standard assumptions and mark affected areas accordingly.

transition.headline: Setting the table.
transition.body: We're laying out your drafting board — one moment.

errors.insertFailed: Project couldn't be created. Please try again.
errors.primingFailed: We couldn't reach the team. Please try again.

pdf.body: Complete summary as a printable PDF.

system.label: System note
system.fallbackTemplateNotice: You picked "Other". We start with the standard single-family-home template and adapt it as we talk.

needs_designer.body: At this point we need a certified architect authorised to submit applications. You can share the project now or pause it for the moment.

blocked.body: We need further input to continue. Please complete the flagged areas or pause the project.

input.freeTextHint: You can also reply in your own words.

attachment.title: Attach a file
attachment.comingSoon: File upload for plans, photos and PDFs lands in the next update. You'll soon be able to attach existing drawings, development plans, and land registry extracts directly to the consultation.

address.helper: We use the address to research the development plan and local statutes.
address.invalid: Please enter a full address with house number and postal code.

idk.label: I'm not sure
idk.researchExplain: We check available sources and mark the result as an assumption.
idk.assumeExplain: We start with a typical case and can correct it later.
idk.skipExplain: We park this question; it doesn't block anything essential.

chat.preliminaryFooter: Preliminary — confirmed by a certified architect.
